headline: US filings for jobless aid tick up last week to 210,000 but remain at historically healthy levels
summary: The number of Americans applying for jobless aid inched up last week as employers continue to retain workers despite a labor market that has weakened considerably in the past year.  U.S. applications for jobless aid for the week ending March 21 rose by 5,000 to 210,000 from the previous week’s 205,000, the Labor Department reported Thursday.  Filings for unemployment benefits are considered representative of U.S. layoffs and are close to a real-time indicator of the health of the job market.

content.title: US filings for jobless aid tick up last week to 210,000 but remain at historically healthy levels

Filings for unemployment benefits are considered representative of U.S. layoffs and are close to a real-time indicator of the health of the job market.

headline: Latest News In E-Commerce - WeShop Empowers Creators with UK Founders Programme Launch
summary: WeShop Holdings Limited has announced the launch of its Founders Programme in the United Kingdom, aiming to empower early community creators and enhance the growth of its community-owned social commerce platform. This initiative is designed to involve a select group of 1,000 early members who will receive benefits such as doubled referral rates and invitations to exclusive events. The programme, which aligns with WeShop's vision of community ownership, seeks to accelerate community-led growth...

headline: Chip Selloff Deepens After Google Touts Memory Breakthrough
summary: (Bloomberg) -- Memory chip stocks extended their losses on Thursday after Alphabet Inc.’s Google publicized research that could allow more efficient use of the storage needed for artificial intelligence development.SK Hynix Inc. and Samsung Electronics Co., South Korean leaders in the market, fell more than 6% and about 5% in Seoul. In the US, Micron Technology Inc., Western Digital Corp. and Sandisk Corp. slid more than 2% in pre-market trading, after they all closed lower on Wednesday. Memory
